title: Genomic DNA extraction from sea cucumber body-wall tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"loe","text":"This is a DNA extraction protocol from sea cucumber (Apostichopus japonicus) body-wall tissue. It accompanies the following GigaScience publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":124,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b9b3u","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3cfb2","text":"Jihoon Jo, et al.  (2016): Draft genome of the sea cucumber Apostichopus japonicus and genetic polymorphism among color variants. GigaScience...","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":130,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6flv6","text":"Pre-heat the waterbath or hot-plate to lysis buffer incubation, and pre-heat the CTAB lysis buffer at 65℃.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6quk0","text":"Phenol, Chloroform and Isopropanol are toxic. Please read the MSDS before working with this chemical.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissect the body-wall tissue (about 3~5 g) and cut them in small pieces with a razor blade. Rinse the tissues with autoclaved sea-water for minimalize the bacterial contamination.
Add tissues in mortar with liquid nitrogen and grinding tissue.

Transfer ground tissues in a 35ml centrifuge tube with 10ml of pre-heated CTAB lysis buffer, add Protease K in the mix and incubate it at 65℃ for 1hour with mixing by gentle inverting every 5-10 min. 01:00:00
Add 10ml of Phenol solution and incubate at 65℃ for 10min. 00:10:00
Add 10ml of Chloroform and mix by gently inverting at RT for 5min. 00:05:00
Transfer the supermatant aqueous phase in a new tube
Add 10ml of Chloroform and mix by gently inverting at RT for 5min. 00:05:00
Transfer the supermatant aqueous phase in a new tube, add the same volume of Isopropanol and 1/3 volume of 7.5M ammonium acetate. 7.5M ammonium acetate can be added to 0.5 volume of supernatant.
Gently mix the tube and incubate the tube at -80℃ for overnight. 16:00:00
Centrifuge at 12000xg for 10min at 4℃. 00:10:00 Do not touch the DNA pellet.
Add 70% of ethanol for washing the DNA pellet.
Centrifuge at 12000xg for 10min at 4℃. 00:10:00 Do not touch the DNA pellet.
Discard the ethanol and air-dry the pellet for 10-20min at RT. 00:20:00
Add 1-2ml of TE buffer (10mM Tris, 1mM EDTA) and dissolve the pellet. Heat the tube at 60℃ for completely dissolve the pellet.
